hero.subtitle: Make faster, safer clinical decisions with specialized calculators, evidence-based clinical guidelines, and an AI trained on official medical literature.
hero.trust_badge: Trusted by over 2,000 professionals in ophthalmology
hero.mockup_answer: The Hoyt sign refers to retinal nerve fiber layer defects visible on fundoscopy, indicating axonal loss in the optic nerve. It is a finding of glaucomatous optic neuropathy.

lens.mockup_question: Red eye and pain. How to differentiate episcleritis from scleritis?
lens.mockup_answer: If there is only mild discomfort, superficial hyperemia, and vessels that blanch with phenylephrine, the presentation is consistent with episcleritis — a benign and self-limited condition. If there is intense, deep pain, diffuse hyperemia with a violaceous appearance, and vessels that do not blanch with phenylephrine, the diagnosis is scleritis — a potentially serious disease requiring systemic treatment.
lens.mockup_citation: CBO: Diseases of the Sclera, p. 187

faq.title: Get Your Questions Answered
faq.desc: Everything you need to know about Oftbook before getting started.
faq.q1: Is Oftbook safe for making clinical decisions?
faq.a1: Yes. All clinical guidelines and Lens AI responses are grounded in official ophthalmological scientific literature. The platform is developed by ophthalmologists and continuously reviewed by specialists in the field.
faq.q2: Can Oftbook's AI hallucinate or fabricate answers?
faq.a2: Lens AI was trained exclusively on ophthalmology content validated by specialists. Unlike general-purpose AI systems, it does not fabricate responses. When it lacks sufficient information on a topic, it informs the user rather than generating imprecise content.
faq.q3: Are the clinical guidelines updated frequently?
faq.a3: Yes. Our team of specialists continuously reviews and updates the more than 300 clinical guidelines available on the platform, keeping pace with the latest scientific evidence and ophthalmology society guidelines.
faq.q4: Does Oftbook work on mobile phones and tablets?
faq.a4: Yes. Oftbook is fully responsive and optimized for smartphones, tablets, and desktops. The interface automatically adapts to screen size, ensuring a smooth experience on any device.
faq.q5: Do I need an internet connection to use it?
faq.a5: Yes, Oftbook is an online platform that requires an internet connection to access content, ensuring you always have the most up-to-date information. The platform is lightweight and performs well even on slower connections.
faq.q6: In which countries is Oftbook available?
faq.a6: Oftbook is available globally; however, the content is focused on ophthalmology practice and Brazilian regulations. Professionals from other Portuguese-speaking countries may also benefit from the platform.
faq.a7: We offer specialized support via email and WhatsApp. Our team is prepared to assist with technical questions about the platform and to complement clinical inquiries as well.
faq.q8: Can I cancel my subscription at any time?
faq.a8: Yes. You may cancel your subscription at each billing cycle, with no penalties or additional fees. We also offer a 7-day unconditional guarantee: if you are not satisfied, we refund 100% of your investment.
faq.a9: Oftbook was created for ophthalmologists, physicians in training, residents, and medical students who want a reliable tool for clinical and surgical decision-making — whether in private practice, hospital settings, or during residency.
faq.q10: What is included in the subscription?
faq.a10: The subscription provides full access to all features: over 300 clinical guidelines, 8+ specialized calculators, Lens AI (ophthalmology AI), ophthalmology drug formulary, atlas with detailed clinical images, surgical guides, CID-10/CID-11 codes, and all future updates.
faq.contact_text: Still have questions? Speak directly with our team.
